title: [Bug]: podman cannot read GPG key on MacOS
body:
### Issue Description

```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ uname -a
Darwin MacBook-Pro 21.6.0 Darwin Kernel Version 21.6.0: Mon Dec 19 20:43:09 PST 2022; root:xnu-8020.240.18~2/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.18.8
Built:        Thu Nov 10 04:43:58 2022
OS/Arch:      darwin/arm64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.3.1
API Version:  4.3.1
Go Version:   go1.19.2
Built:        Fri Nov 11 23:00:31 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: Source image rejected: Invalid GPG signature: gpgme.Signature{Summary:128, Fingerprint:"1AC4971355A34A82", Status:gpgme.Error{err:0x9}, Timestamp:time.Date(2023, time.February, 4, 6, 12, 51, 0, time.Local), ExpTimestamp:time.Date(1970, time.January, 1, 8, 0, 0, 0, time.Local), WrongKeyUsage:false, PKATrust:0x0, ChainModel:false, Validity:0, ValidityReason:error(nil), PubkeyAlgo:1, HashAlgo:8}
```

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Import GPG public key.
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ gpg --import isv_gpg.txt 
gpg: key 1AC4971355A34A82: public key "Red Hat, Inc. (ISV Container Signing Key) <secalert@redhat.com>" imported
gpg: Total number processed: 1
gpg:               imported: 1

MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ gpg --check-signatures
/Users/jianzhang/.gnupg/pubring.kbx
-----------------------------------
pub   rsa2048 2022-06-07 [SC]
      CF26F728049E8A274A2BB506F4439BB7D638D0B7
uid           [ultimate] jiazha (use it for private log downloading) <jiazha@redhat.com>
sig!3        F4439BB7D638D0B7 2022-06-07  jiazha (use it for private log downloading) <jiazha@redhat.com>
sub   rsa2048 2022-06-07 [E]
sig!         F4439BB7D638D0B7 2022-06-07  jiazha (use it for private log downloading) <jiazha@redhat.com>

pub   rsa4096 2019-08-30 [SC]
      DC1FEDE4C5BD3225F7E912751AC4971355A34A82
uid           [ unknown] Red Hat, Inc. (ISV Container Signing Key) <secalert@redhat.com>
sig!3        1AC4971355A34A82 2019-08-30  Red Hat, Inc. (ISV Container Signing Key) <secalert@redhat.com>

gpg: 3 good signatures
```
2. Pull that ISV image with `pdoman`, but still failed at:
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: Source image rejected: Invalid GPG signature: gpgme.Signature{Summary:128, Fingerprint:"1AC4971355A34A82", Status:gpgme.Error{err:0x9}, Timestamp:time.Date(2023, time.February, 4, 6, 12, 51, 0, time.Local), ExpTimestamp:time.Date(1970, time.January, 1, 8, 0, 0, 0, time.Local), WrongKeyUsage:false, PKATrust:0x0, ChainModel:false, Validity:0, ValidityReason:error(nil), PubkeyAlgo:1, HashAlgo:8}
```


### Describe the results you received

```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12
Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: Source image rejected: Invalid GPG signature: gpgme.Signature{Summary:128, Fingerprint:"1AC4971355A34A82", Status:gpgme.Error{err:0x9}, Timestamp:time.Date(2023, time.February, 4, 6, 12, 51, 0, time.Local), ExpTimestamp:time.Date(1970, time.January, 1, 8, 0, 0, 0, time.Local), WrongKeyUsage:false, PKATrust:0x0, ChainModel:false, Validity:0, ValidityReason:error(nil), PubkeyAlgo:1, HashAlgo:8}
```

### Describe the results you expected

The private image can be pulled successfully after setting the gpg key.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

I also re-init the `podman` VM to have a try, but still failed, details:
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ podman machine list
NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED       LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE

require root permissions (e.g. ports < 1024), or if you run into compatibility
issues with non-podman clients, you can switch using the following command:

The system helper service is not installed; the default Docker API socket
address can't be used by podman. If you would like to install it run the

You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER_HOST using the
following command in your terminal session:

NAME                     VM TYPE     CREATED             LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE

Trying to pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12...
Error: copying system image from manifest list: Source image rejected: Invalid GPG signature: gpgme.Signature{Summary:128, Fingerprint:"1AC4971355A34A82", Status:gpgme.Error{err:0x9}, Timestamp:time.Date(2023, time.February, 4, 6, 12, 51, 0, time.Local), ExpTimestamp:time.Date(1970, time.January, 1, 8, 0, 0, 0, time.Local), WrongKeyUsage:false, PKATrust:0x0, ChainModel:false, Validity:0, ValidityReason:error(nil), PubkeyAlgo:1, HashAlgo:8}
```

comments.body:
FYI, `docker` works well.
```yaml
MacBook-Pro:~ jianzhang$ docker pull registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12
v4.12: Pulling from redhat/certified-operator-index
f1543f27d131: Pull complete 
5d5771e51f28: Pull complete 
4459419314e3: Pull complete 
f99e1652940a: Pull complete 
593a8c86851a: Pull complete 
8a6ace4d247b: Pull complete 
Digest: sha256:5ad35ac1ec62e5a0e3a6e0de60d813368c2e9ab786d5408ca6bef77f15b26f21
Status: Downloaded newer image for registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.12
```

Note that the podman pull runs inside the linux VM so I doubt that importing the gpg key on your mac can help.

@vrothberg @mtrmac PTAL

- Podman signature verification does not use the per-user GNUPGHOME at all: the trusted keys are defined in `policy.json` (either directly the contents, or references to paths on that same machine). So I would expect `gpg --import` to have no effect.
- I think you’ll find, in the relevant `policy.json`, that the ISV key is not trusted by default for Red Hat registries. You can add a more specific scope (for the specific images that are signed by the ISV key) where that key is trusted.
- I’m not immediately sure whether the relevant `policy.json` is the one on the client or on the remote server, I’d guess the remote server. Changing the policy to use `"type": "reject"` for the relevant image would be a quick way to check.

Hi @Luap99 and @mtrmac, thanks for explanations, but one more question, where is the `policy.json` location for MacOS? Seems like it doesn't support the `trust` command. If yes, how can I use it on MacOS? Thanks!

- MacOS

```yaml
MacBook-Pro:.local jianzhang$ podman image trust set --help
Error: cannot use command "podman image trust set" with the remote podman client
```

- RHEL

```yaml
[cloud-user@preserve-olm-env2 ~]$ podman image trust set --help     
Set default trust policy or a new trust policy for a registry

Description:
  Set default trust policy or add a new trust policy for a registry

Usage:
  podman image trust set [options] REGISTRY

Options:
  -f, --pubkeysfile strings   Path of installed public key(s) to trust for TARGET.
                              Absolute path to keys is added to policy.json. May
                              used multiple times to define multiple public keys.
                              File(s) must exist before using this command
  -t, --type string           Trust type, accept values: signedBy(default), accept, reject (default "signedBy")
```
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
policy.json file is inside of the VM not on the MAC Client.
